channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Federal employees may get more demands to justify their work at Elon Musk's direction
item.description: The turmoil that enveloped the federal workforce over the last few days is unlikely to cease anytime soon.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to unfreeze funds for US foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court to ease its funding freeze. U.S. District Judge Amir H.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rules against drivers in a case advocates say could make civil-rights claims harder
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court said Tuesday that people who win early rulings in civil rights cases won't necessarily be able to recover their legal fees, a finding that both conservative and liberal groups had argued could make it harder to fight for people's rights in court.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Live updates: White House to face questions after DOGE email confusion
item.description: House Republican leadership is working to pass a budget resolution on Tuesday to pave the way for President Trump's agenda, but opposition has emerged from all sides of the conference.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5

item.title: Noem reveals major milestone on border crossings amid Trump's crackdown on illegal immigrants
item.description: Encounters at the southern border hit a record 15-year record low, Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson tries to push Trump's 'big' agenda forward, but GOP votes are in jeopardy
item.description: House Speaker Mike Johnson will try against the odds to muscle a Republican budget blueprint to passage this week.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro, Kevin Freking And Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Deadline for Musk's ultimatum to federal workers hits, but consequences remain vague
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk says federal employees will get a second chance to respond to work accountability requests after Monday night's deadline passed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judicial pushback on Trump 'hurting American people,' says GOP rep leading impeachment charge
item.description: GOP Rep. Andrew Clyde spoke with Fox News Digital about drafting impeachment articles against Judge John McConnell and his efforts to hold judges accountable amid pushback on Trump policies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Pentagon leadership shakeup aims to recapture 'warrior ethos,' expert says
item.description: President Donald Trump's move to fire multiple top military officials has reportedly caused "upheaval" at the Pentagon, but should help the president carry out his agenda.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly 40% of contracts canceled by Musk's DOGE are expected to produce no savings
item.description: Nearly 40% of the federal contracts that President Donald Trump’s administration claims to have canceled as part of its signature cost-cutting program aren’t expected to save the government any money, the administration’s data shows.
item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Foley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump expressed hope that Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he met Monday with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the invasion.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s Cabinet members have already backtracked on some promises made before being confirmed
item.description: As they mustered support for their confirmations by the U.S. Senate, some of President Donald Trump’s appointees made statements from which they’ve already distanced themselves upon taking office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: St. Pete lawmaker proposes bill to dive into wastewater, sewage spill concerns
item.description: The devastating impacts of storm surge and flood waters are a harsh reality many Floridians know all too well.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New FBI Director Kash Patel is sworn in as acting ATF chief, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — New FBI Director Kash Patel was sworn in Monday as acting chief of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, taking the helm of two separate and sprawling Justice Department agencies, according to a person familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ron DeSantis talks up his wife as next Florida governor and takes a shot at Trump's pick
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — As Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis approaches the end of his second term in office, he’s talking up one potential heir to succeed him in 2026: his wife, Casey DeSantis. And he's taking a shot at President Donald Trump 's pick to be the next governor.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A new document undercuts Trump admin's denials about $400 million Tesla deal
item.description: The State Department claimed a plan to buy thousands of armored Teslas was left over from the Biden administration. A document obtained by NPR shows the Biden plan was far smaller.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: In window before special election, House Republicans push for votes on their agenda
item.description: With a slim majority, Republicans in the Minnesota House are pushing forward bills that are likely not to pass a floor vote. They hope it will still send a political message.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox and Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Nonprofit working on Mississippi River project loses federal funding
item.description: Mississippi Park Connection said funds for restoration work that has not yet been completed are frozen, as are funds for congressionally funded work that has already been completed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: The Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The Supreme Court on Friday temporarily kept on the job the head of the federal agency that protects government whistleblowers, in its first word on the many legal fights over President Donald Trump’s second-term agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Judge cancels trial for NYC Mayor Eric Adams but leaves corruption charges intact for now
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday cancelled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise him on how to handle the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Planned medical research grant cuts worry Sarasota scientists
item.description: For more than 20 years, Sarasota-based biomedical research facility The Roskamp Institute has been focused on understanding and finding treatment, prevention, and early detection for Alzheimer's disease and other dementia, along with illnesses facing veterans.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cortez Masto, Rosen introduce proposal to block Trump plan to end birthright citizenship
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sens. Catherine Cortez Masto and Jacky Rosen introduced a proposal Thursday to block President Donald Trump's attempt to end the constitutional right to birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Florida Democrats file bill to repeal 6-week abortion ban
item.description: House Minority Leader Fentrice Driskell, who represents families in Hillsborough County, said the legislation comes because it’s a mandate from voters.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gov. Gavin Newsom proposes $125M mortgage relief program to help victims of recent natural disasters
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom on Wednesday proposed a new $125 million package to help residents whose homes were destroyed or damaged by recent natural disasters, including the recent wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Florida cabinet assumes new role, sparking criticism over privacy and power
item.description: After a month-long feud over who would be the state's chief immigration enforcement officer, state lawmakers passed a bill package that grants not one, but four elected officials with that task.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
